text: Laparoscopic exploration reveals a fistula between the terminal ileum and the sigmoid colon in a 52-year-old woman with Crohn's disease.

The surgeon uses a combination of sharp and blunt dissection techniques to detach the fistula between the terminal ileum and the sigmoid colon, following the preoperative colonoscopy findings of no mucosal colonic disease.
The surgeon carefully dissects the fistula, focusing on the terminal ileum side, which is intended to be part of the removed specimen.

The surgeon approximates the defect in the sigmoid mesocolon using intracorporeal sutures. A flexible sigmoidoscopy is performed to visually inspect for any full-thickness defects in the sigmoid colon and to conduct an air-water leak test.
The surgeon develops the retrohalial plane and completes the ileocecal resection using a medial approach, fully mobilizing the hepatic flexure.
The surgeon identifies the duodenum, pancreas, and gallbladder through the medial window and proceeds with the full mobilization of the hepatic flexure from below.
The surgeon performs an end-to-end ileocolic anastomosis on the chronically dilated small bowel, using hands-on techniques to connect the ileum to the colon.
